title: Eria bracteolata
taxonomy: Eria bracteolata is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. It is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which comprises the majority of orchid species. More specifically, Eria bracteolata is placed within the subfamily Epidendroideae. This subfamily is further divided into several tribes, with Eria bracteolata belonging to the tribe Vandeae. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it generally falls under the mentioned family and subfamily.

side_effects: Eria bracteolata, a plant traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, has been reported to have potential side effects due to its toxicity. intro: Eria bracteolata, also known as the Bracteolate Eria, is a species of orchid native to tropical forests in Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and fever, due to its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Eria bracteolata include flavonoids, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Eria bracteolata include decoctions, infusions, and capsules, which are used to relieve coughs, colds, and other respiratory afflictions.
